owner.description: Repository owner (user or organization login). Legacy accepted a single "owner/repo" repository config value and split it at runtime; the engine's path templating urlencodes each {{ }} reference as one opaque path segment (a literal "/" would be percent-encoded, not treated as a segment delimiter), so owner and repo are declared as two separate config keys instead.

repo.description: Repository name (without the owner prefix). See owner's description for why this is a separate config key from legacy's single repository field.

base_url.description: GitHub API base URL override for tests or GitHub Enterprise.

installation_id.description: GitHub App installation ID for auth_type=github_app.

installation_repositories.description: Optional comma-separated repository names for a restricted installation token.

installation_repository_ids.description: Optional comma-separated repository IDs for a restricted installation token.

installation_permissions.description: Optional JSON object of requested GitHub App installation-token permissions.

public_access.description: Explicit opt-in for unauthenticated (public) reads. Set to any non-empty value (e.g. 'true') to allow reads with no token/app credentials configured. Leaving this unset requires a token secret or app_id+installation_id+private_key to authenticate; a config with none of those AND public_access unset hard-errors rather than silently falling back to an unauthenticated read (F4 fail-loud deviation from legacy's auth_type=auto resolution, which DOES silently fall through to public — see docs.md Known limits/ledger G14). Legacy's auth_type=public/none/anonymous/unauthenticated string-value selection is NOT reproduced 1:1 (the engine's `when` grammar has no bare string-equality/membership check statically validated by connectorgen today) — this bundle uses a dedicated boolean-shaped opt-in key instead; the resulting behavior (explicit opt-in required, no silent fallthrough) is what the legacy behavior SHOULD have been from a fail-loud standpoint, and is strictly safer, never less capable for any legacy caller who genuinely wants a public read.

since.description: Lower-bound timestamp for issues, issue_comments, and pull_request_review_comments (incremental start; also usable as a fresh-sync start_config_key value).

token.description: GitHub token: classic PAT, fine-grained PAT, OAuth token, GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN, or a pre-generated installation access token. Used only for Bearer auth; never logged.

private_key.description: GitHub App PEM private key for auth_type=github_app. Never logged.

private_key_base64.description: Base64-encoded GitHub App PEM private key for auth_type=github_app (alternative to private_key). Never logged.
